````instructions
---
applyTo: "**"
---

# Build Guide — RC-Servo (CH32V203 / CH32L103 / STM32C091)

## Tools

| Tool | Path |
|------|------|
| CMake + Ninja | Via system PATH |
| RISC-V GCC | `sdk/xpack-riscv-none-elf-gcc-14.2.0-3/bin/` |
| ARM GCC | System PATH (for STM32C091 builds) |

---

## Architecture

Three firmware components sharing a common bootloader architecture:

| Component | Description | Flash Region |
|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| **Bootloader** | Secure boot with ECDSA P-256 signature verification | `0x08000000` (16 KB) |
| **Application** | Main application firmware | `0x08004000` (40 KB) |
| **BL Updater** | Bootloader updater (runs from app slot) | `0x08004000` (40 KB) |

The bl_updater embeds the bootloader binary via `incbin` — the bootloader MUST be built first.

---

## 1. Build (Debug — CH32V203)

```powershell
cmake --build --preset ch32v203-debug
```

Or press `Ctrl+Shift+B` in VS Code (default build task).

This builds all three components: bootloader, application, and bl_updater.

### Build Outputs

| Component | ELF Path | BIN Path |
|-----------|----------|----------|
| Bootloader | `build/ch32v203-debug/bootloader/bootloader.elf` | (generate via objcopy) |
| Application | `build/ch32v203-debug/application/application.elf` | (generate via objcopy) |
| BL Updater | `build/ch32v203-debug/bl_updater/bl_updater.elf` | (generate via objcopy) |

### Generate .bin from .elf

```powershell
& "sdk/xpack-riscv-none-elf-gcc-14.2.0-3/bin/riscv-none-elf-objcopy.exe" `
    -O binary build/ch32v203-debug/bootloader/bootloader.elf `
    build/ch32v203-debug/bootloader/bootloader.bin
```

---

## 2. Build Presets

| Preset | Platform | Build Type | Toolchain |
|--------|----------|------------|-----------|
| `ch32v203` | CH32V203 | Release | RISC-V GCC |
| `ch32v203-debug` | CH32V203 | Debug (`-Og -g`) | RISC-V GCC |
| `ch32l103` | CH32L103 | Release | RISC-V GCC |
| `stm32c091` | STM32C091 | Release | ARM GCC |

### Configure (first time or after CMake changes)

```powershell
cmake --preset ch32v203-debug
```

The `RISCV_TOOLCHAIN_PATH` environment variable must be set. The VS Code build task sets it automatically.

---

## 3. Flash Memory Map

```
Address         Size    Region
────────────────────────────────────────
0x08000000      16 KB   Bootloader
0x08004000      40 KB   Application
0x0800E000       4 KB   Factory Data (signed, UID hash)
0x0800F000       4 KB   User Data (app runtime storage)
────────────────────────────────────────
0x20000000      20 KB   RAM
```

Physical flash at `0x00000000` is aliased to `0x08000000`. Both access the same memory.

---

## 4. Common Build Errors

**Missing toolchain**  Set `RISCV_TOOLCHAIN_PATH` or use the VS Code build task which sets it automatically.

**Configuration stale**  Run `cmake --preset ch32v203-debug` again before rebuild.

**Ninja error: file not found**  Delete the `build/ch32v203-debug/` folder, re-run configure + build.

**incbin bootloader.bin not found**  The bl_updater depends on the bootloader binary. Build the bootloader first (the preset builds all components in the correct order).

---

## 5. Signing Firmware

The bootloader verifies ECDSA P-256 signatures. Test artifacts are generated by:

```powershell
py -3 Target/test_bootloader_setup.py --uid <12-byte-hex-uid>
```

Key files in `keys/`:

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `private_key.pem` | Firmware signing key |
| `fd_private_key.pem` | Factory data signing key |
| `public_key_c.h` | Public key embedded in bootloader |
| `fd_public_key_c.h` | FD public key embedded in bootloader |
````
